questions.question: What is the FASP Quality Gate for AI-drafted emails?

options.text: Factual (verified references), Actually relevant (connected to value prop), Specific to this person (not generic), Proud if they knew how you found it
First line, Ask, Social proof, Pitch

questions.explanation: Every AI-drafted email must pass FASP: Factual (all references verified — no hallucinations), Actually relevant (not a stretch to your value prop), Specific to this person (not applicable to anyone), and Proud if they knew how you found the information (not creepy or invasive).

questions.question: How many email variants does the Email First-Draft Agent generate per prospect, and why?

options.text: 3 variants (pain-focused, trigger-focused, value-focused) to give options for different angles
No variants — the AI sends the best one automatically

questions.explanation: The agent generates 3 variants per prospect: pain-focused (addresses their challenge), trigger-focused (references a recent event), and value-focused (leads with your solution's benefit). This gives you options without overwhelm, and each variant can be adapted to different sequence positions.

questions.question: What is the '80/20 Rule of AI Email Drafting'?

options.text: 80% of emails should be AI, 20% should be human
AI gets you to 80-90% quality in 30 seconds; the human edit adds the final 10-20% that makes the difference between 3% and 8% reply rates

questions.explanation: AI gets you to 80-90% quality in 30 seconds. The human edit — fixing an awkward phrase, sharpening the CTA, adding a personal touch — adds the final 10-20% that makes the difference between 3% reply rates (pure AI) and 8% reply rates (AI + human polish).

questions.question: Treating AI like a magic button ('write me a cold email') produces high-quality output without any additional context or constraints.
questions.explanation: False. 'Write me a cold email' produces generic garbage. The secret is in prompt architecture — feeding the AI your research brief, voice guide, proven templates, and strict constraints that prevent hallucination and spam. Quality output requires quality input and structure.

questions.question: Take a real prospect from your list and describe the inputs you would feed to your Email First-Draft Agent (research brief, voice guide, template constraints). Then draft what you think the agent's best variant would look like and explain which FASP criteria it passes.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response includes specific agent inputs (research data, voice constraints, template structure), produces an actual email draft (not just a description), and evaluates it against all four FASP criteria. Look for understanding that prompt architecture drives output quality.
